channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: La búsqueda de una conspiración desencadenó una crisis en el Departamento de Justicia
item.description: El impulso para investigar lo que los aliados de Trump consideraban una conspiración del “Estado profundo” que pretendía destituir al presidente desencadenó una crisis en cascada, acabó con carreras profesionales y socavó la credibilidad del Departamento ante los jueces.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:description: El gran caso de conspiración del director del FBI, Kash Patel, buscaba vincular las acciones de un grupo de personas a las que el presidente Donald Trump culpaba de varias investigaciones en su contra.

item.title: Wemby and the Spurs Bounce Back to Win Game 3
item.description: How did the San Antonio Spurs pull it off?
item.dc:creator: Dave DuFour, Es Baraheni and Andrew Schlecht

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times

item.title: Spurs Beat Knicks, Quieting New York City Crowds
item.description: Fans, celebrities and President Trump gathered at Madison Square Garden in New York City to cheer on the Knicks, who lost Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals to the San Antonio Spurs.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times

item.title: 5 consejos para ayudar a gestionar el dinero de tus padres mayores
item.description: Para muchas familias, la conversación sobre la situación económica de los padres ancianos suele llegar demasiado tarde. Es mejor prepararse.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Rabasca Roepe

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cathleen Tobin, planificadora financiera, ha estado ayudando a su padre, Ed, con su dinero. “Ayudar a tus padres en pequeñas cosas genera confianza y normaliza tu participación”, dijo.

item.title: He Gave It All to Help the Knicks Win in 1973. He’s Still in Pain Today.
item.description: Earl “the Pearl” Monroe was part of the last championship team. Now 81, he has thoughts on what really matters.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein

media:content.media:description: In the deciding game of the 1973 N.B.A. championship, Earl Monroe drives past a Laker. The Knicks won their second title that night, May 10, 1973.

item.title: Medical Advances Are Keeping a Health Reporter Busy
item.description: A conversation with Gina Kolata, who covers breakthroughs in medical science.
item.dc:creator: Terence McGinley

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Karsten Moran for The New York Times, Nathan Bajar for The New York Times, NIAID, via Associated Press, Liz Hafalia/The San Francisco Chronicle, via Getty Images, Kim Raff for The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 9, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Late Night Roasts Trump for Walking Out on ‘Meet the Press’
item.description: Jon Stewart called the president’s interview his “worst nightmare: a woman who won’t stop asking pertinent questions.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)

item.title: Las obras de Frida Kahlo vuelven a brillar en el Museo Dolores Olmedo
item.description: La gran mecenas de Diego Rivera, Dolores Olmedo, consideraba a Frida como una rival, pero compró 26 de sus obras de arte, la mayor colección. Sus obras se exponen en Ciudad de México.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Burnett and Pablo López Luz

item.title: Gusano barrenador en EE. UU.: qué han hecho las autoridades
item.description: Los funcionarios están evaluando conceder una autorización de emergencia de moscas estériles genéticamente modificadas para controlar las poblaciones del insecto parásito carnívoro.
item.dc:creator: K. R. Callaway and Emily Anthes

media:content.media:description: La mosca del gusano barrenador del Nuevo Mundo. Pone huevos en heridas abiertas u orificios de animales de sangre caliente; las larvas resultantes excavan en la herida y la expanden para consumir el tejido del animal.

item.title: Word of the Day: abnegate
item.description: This word has appeared in three articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Night With the Knicks: Rancor, Jubilation and an Unusual Loss
item.description: The night began with frustration and boos, and not because of the Knicks. But by the end of Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals, the team had slightly deflated some spirits, too.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:description: President Trump’s presence at Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals at Madison Square Garden shifted the vibe both inside and outside the arena.

item.title: Alcohol’s Health Risks Accelerate After One Drink a Day, Study Finds
item.description: The alcohol industry has criticized the research, which found that even light drinking increases the risk of premature death.
item.dc:creator: Roni Caryn Rabin

media:content.media:credit: Cedric Angeles for The New York Times

item.title: Meet Europe’s New Machiavelli
item.description: Luuk van Middelaar, a Dutch scholar, is emerging as the continent’s master strategist.
item.dc:creator: Anton Jäger

item.title: Trump Booed at Game 3 of N.B.A. Finals
item.description: Fans at Madison Square Garden and watch parties around New York City booed when President Trump was shown attending Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:description: President Trump was booed when he appeared on the video board during Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals in New York.

item.title: Lo que sabemos sobre los medicamentos para perder peso cambia rápidamente
item.description: Gracias al uso generalizado de fármacos como el Ozempic, ahora tenemos una idea más clara que nunca de sus efectos y de las dificultades que conlleva tomarlos.
item.dc:creator: Dani Blum

item.title: Why a U.K. By-Election in Makerfield Could Topple Prime Minister Keir Starmer
item.description: Andy Burnham, the mayor of Greater Manchester, is running for a seat in Parliament. It’s a vital step in a campaign to oust his party colleague Prime Minister Keir Starmer.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:description: Andy Burnham launching his special election campaign last month. He was a member of Parliament for more than a decade before becoming mayor of Greater Manchester.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Some Trump Voters Are Drifting Away Over His Handling of Iran and Economy
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, June 9, 2026.

item.title: Corrections: June 9, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, June 9, 2026.

item.title: Crew Rescued After U.S. Helicopter Goes Down Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It was not immediately clear what led to the incident, which came at a time when the cease-fire in the war has grown tenuous.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A U.S. Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: See the Famous Faces at the Knicks Game at Madison Square Garden
item.description: Here’s a look at some of the most prominent figures that attended Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals at Madison Square Garden.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb, Bora Erden, Marco Hernandez and Jon Huang

item.title: San Francisco Voters Appear to Reject Tax Hike on Highly Paid C.E.O.s
item.description: The proposal was seen as a possible measure of how residents felt about the recent flood of money into the city as a result of the A.I. boom.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla

media:content.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Updated voting results on Monday showed that about 46 percent of voters had approved the measure.

media:content.media:credit: Miles Cohen for The New York Times

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 9, 2026
item.description: Rebecca Goldstein’s puzzle isn’t too tough to dig into.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:description: Read the Wordplay column. Just ink case.

item.title: His Mayor’s Muslim. His Bagel’s Jewish. His Words Are New York.
item.description: MD Ahnaf Hossain, 23, delivered a four-line instant classic to a TV camera after a Knicks win, and it has since rocketed across social media. “I had to say what was in my heart,” he said in an interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Maslin Nir

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Four lines celebrating the diversity of New York, and the Knicks, have become a rallying cry across the city during the N.B.A. finals.

item.title: M.S.G. Erupts in Boos as Trump Appears on Screen at Knicks-Spurs Game
item.description: Mr. Trump is the first sitting president to attend an N.B.A. finals game. He smiled as he heard the reception from the crowd at Madison Square Garden.
item.dc:creator: Tania Ganguli

media:content.media:description: President Trump was booed when he appeared on the video board during Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals in New York.

item.title: For Lonely Knicks Fans, Championship Run Forges Fellowship in NYC
item.description: The championship run by the Knicks has created a “love fest,” one fan said, and it has even given her a new circle of friends.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

media:content.media:description: Jennifer Shoemaker before the Knicks N.B.A. finals Game 2 watch party at Madison Square Garden.

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times

item.title: Unpacking the Famous Words in the Declaration of Independence
item.description: Our critic A.O. Scott breaks down the second sentence of the Declaration of Independence.
item.dc:creator: A.O. Scott

media:content.media:credit: Universal History Archive/UIG, via Getty Images

item.title: Frederick Douglass’s Demand for Citizenship Is Still Radical
item.description: In the years after the Civil War, Douglass shared a vision of freedom and equality for all, regardless of race. Our writer Jia Lynn Yang considers that history, and its relevance for the present.
item.dc:creator: Jia Lynn Yang

item.title: ‘Make American Great Again’ Was a Slogan Well Before Trump
item.description: Our critic A.O. Scott explains how four words explain (at least) 40 years of political history.
item.dc:creator: A.O. Scott

item.title: How F.D.R.’s Most Famous Sentence Remade America
item.description: There was plenty to be afraid of in 1933, our chief White House correspondent, Peter Baker, explains. Roosevelt said there was “nothing to fear but fear itself.”
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker

item.title: Nina Simone’s ‘Mississippi Goddam’ Is a Protest Song of the Civil Rights Era, and Our Own
item.description: “Mississippi Goddam” is a song of the Civil Rights Era, and our own. Listen to it with our critic Wesley Morris.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Morris

media:content.media:credit: Alfred Wertheimer/MUUS Collection, via Getty Images

item.title: How Susan B. Anthony Used Language in the Fight for Women’s Rights
item.description: In the fight for women’s rights, language has always been a battleground, our critic Parul Seghal explains.
item.dc:creator: Parul Sehgal

media:content.media:credit: Universal History Archive/UIG, via Getty images

item.title: EE. UU. niega la entrada a un árbitro somalí del Mundial
item.description: Omar Artan fue uno de los 52 árbitros seleccionados para la Copa Mundial de la FIFA. Pero el sábado se le denegó la entrada por “preocupaciones de investigación de antecedentes”, dijeron funcionarios estadounidenses.
item.dc:creator: Hussein Mohamed and Tariq Panja

media:content.media:description: Omar Artan no oficiará partidos en la Copa Mundial, dijo la FIFA.

item.title: As Screwworm Cases Mount, U.S. Officials Ramp Up Response
item.description: The parasitic, flesh-eating fly has now been confirmed in cows, goats and dogs.
item.dc:creator: K. R. Callaway and Emily Anthes

media:content.media:description: The New World screwworm fly. It lays eggs in the open wounds or orifices of warm-blooded animals; the resulting larvae burrow into and expand the wound, consuming the animal’s tissue.

item.title: Timothée Chalamet, Cardi B and More Celebrities Are at Knicks-Spurs Finals Game 3
item.description: A long list of celebrities flocked to Madison Square Garden for Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Keh

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Musicians DJ Khaled and Fat Joe are sitting courtside for the first game of the N.B.A. finals at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Starlink’s Brilliant Marketing Strategy
item.description: The “Hard Fork” co-hosts Kevin Roose and Casey Newton discuss how recent United flights have caused them to change their minds about Starlink.

item.title: I.C.C. Prosecutor Karim Khan Suspended Over Sexual Harassment Claims
item.description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court, faced an accusation of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek and Amanda Taub

media:content.media:description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court.

item.title: Nithya Raman Secures Second LA Mayor Spot, Ending Spencer Pratt’s Run
item.description: Nithya Raman, a progressive Democrat who entered the mayoral race at the last minute, secured the second spot to face Mayor Karen Bass in November. Her comeback sets up an intraparty battle.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler

item.title: Wonder What a Renovated Penn Station Might Look Like? Here’s a Preview.
item.description: Forget those dreary passageways. The updated transit hub would add a grand entrance with sweeping staircases leading to an airy, glass-walled concourse.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan

media:content.media:description: Today’s Pennsylvania Station is often regarded with contempt by commuters.

item.title: Man Fatally Shot on a Bus in the Bronx
item.description: A 41-year-old man was shot in the abdomen on a Bx36 bus on Monday after getting into an argument with the shooter. The gunman remains at large, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman and Chelsia Rose Marcius

item.title: Would Mike Johnson Pass Raphael Warnock’s Test?
item.description: On “The Interview,” Raphael Warnock, a Democratic senator from Georgia and a pastor, explains his “acid test” on faith.

item.title: Una ‘transformación milagrosa’: cómo Kim Jong-un fortaleció Corea del Norte
item.description: Utilizó la pandemia para reforzar despiadadamente su control sobre el país. Luego dinamizó su economía aprovechando la guerra de Rusia en Ucrania.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

item.title: Governor’s Visit to ICE Detention Facility Is Strictly Limited, She Says
item.description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill of New Jersey said she was not allowed to speak with immigrants at the Delaney Hall detention center.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley and Mark Bonamo

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill of New Jersey had been trying for weeks to tour Delaney Hall, an immigration detention center in Newark.

item.title: Trump Previews Fall Strategy With Baseless Claims of California Vote Fraud
item.description: The president is using the slow count of mail ballots in California to renew his effort to cast doubt on election outcomes he doesn’t like, despite a lack of evidence of any widespread fraud.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan and Maggie Haberman

media:content.media:description: By baselessly framing the rise of a candidate for Los Angeles mayor as a Democratic scam, President Trump has extended his long-running project to erode public faith in elections.

item.title: Three Questions About the Maine and South Carolina Primaries
item.description: Graham Platner and Lindsey Graham are both hoping for comfortably big victories.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

item.title: Knicks Fans Bring Swagger and Nerves to Key Playoff Game
item.description: Madison Square Garden was a cauldron of noise for Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals. New York Knicks fans greeted President Trump with a round of booing.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
